place: Brookfield CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Brookfield’s roots trace back to the early 18th century, when settlers pushed into the area as part of a broader agricultural expansion.
West Jersey proprietors issued land grants in 1726 to pioneers like John C. Axford and George Green, who cleared forests for farming and iron.
White Township was formally created on April 9, 1913, carving Brookfield from Oxford Township and underscoring its rural heritage.
Before 1950, Route 46 and County Route 519 were upgraded to better serve farmers, even as iron operations waned.
During the 1980s and 1990s, roughly 40 % of White Township’s homes were built, largely as age‑restricted communities for retirees.
Most homes in Brookfield are owner‑occupied—about 74 %—with median values near $310,600, reflecting its residential character.
